findings.message: rename_item writes item.name directly but never recalculates the derived slug, so after a rename the persisted slug diverges from its source-of-truth name. The model explicitly documents slug as always derived from name and requiring both to be updated together, and the repository already provides update_item_name() which keeps them in sync. This route bypasses that helper and breaks the derived-field invariant: the unique/indexed slug now no longer matches name.strip().lower().replace(' ', '-'), producing inconsistent data and potentially duplicate-collision/stale-lookup behavior on the unique slug column.
findings.suggestion: Recalculate the slug whenever name changes, or delegate to the existing repository helper: replace the inline mutation with `item = update_item_name(session, item_id, payload.name)` (raising 404 when it returns None), so name and slug are always committed together.
